form: A young woman in her mid-twenties with light blonde, faintly wavy hair cut in curtain bangs, the rest falling to the small of her back — usually worn in a french braid with the bangs left loose. Dark nature-green tank top, brown heavy-material fitted trousers, and natural leather armor pieces worn light for speed. A recurve bow across her back, a dagger at her hip. Something in her eyes is very still and very watchful, like a forest that knows it is being observed.

bio.hometown: She lives in a secret safe haven in a pure nature forest, untouched by nanites. Her mother is an extremely powerful pure nature magic woman, and her father is a pure nanite that urged them to hide, and when nature no longer touched him, he fully succumbed to the nanites and is now an all powerful nanite, not remembering his love, nor his child (Aelyn)
bio.residence: In the hidden forest with her dying mother, and other pure nature people
bio.livelihood: They don't have a financial system
bio.hobbies: patrol, practice archery, make fairy houses, talk to Branch, climb through the trees, write in her journal
bio.motivation: Her mother dies, and she decides to go to the nanite world, to figure out why she is different than the pure nature people.
bio.story:
Aelyn is a young woman in her mid twenties, born of both the worlds of pure nature (her mother) and of the world of pure nanites (her father). she has light blonde, ever so slightly wavy hair, with curtain bangs and the rest of the hair falling to the small of her back. she often wears it in a french braid with her bangs left out. she lives in a secluded, pure nature, hidden refuge in a lush beautiful forest, hidden from the nanites to protect the nature's natural magic. her mother has told her that her father died due to nanite corruption, when in reality he is still alive as an angelic nanite, that has become corrupted by the fabricated power of technology. They loved each other so much, and her father was actually the one who told the pure nature people to hide themselves from the nanites, so their magic wouldn't die. But after they left and he was no longer touched by the magic of nature, the nanites fully corrupted him because it was evolving too much, too fast. Her mother grieved him, in a way even though he didn't die, which greatly hindered her powers, locking them away in a way. 

Aelyn is an expert archer. She is also handy with a dagger and other bladed weapons when necessary. She wears a base outfit of a dark, nature green tank top and tighter, brown slightly thicker material jeans. She has natural crafted leather armor pieces that allow for basic protection but to keep agility to use her bow. She is extremely agile, able to maneuver through trees and all nature foliage with ease and expertise. She has some outside knowledge of the nanite world, keeping up with this small protected civilization's patrol reports. 

Her heritage being of both innate worlds is hidden because she has never left pure nature, so her nanite aspects have been what would be considered dormant. Both her mother and her father are extremely powerful in their respective worlds.  

Aelyn is a grounded, smart woman who is extremely empathetic, but guarded. She isn't scared of the outside world, but rather intimidated by how it consumes the natural world. She craves balance, but also stands by her morals and what's right. Her mother is dying, and she feels lost, unsure of what to do without her. She possesses both great natural, nature magic, and also a hidden, vast nanite power. she has yet to discover both.

chronicleCache.text: You have become someone whose grief is still raw enough to taste — your mother barely cold in the earth when you packed what little you owned and walked away from the only life you knew among the Rootkin, the people of pure nature, the ones who never needed to ask what they were. But you have always needed to ask. The nanite-laced difference in you has been a quiet wrongness your whole life, a hum beneath the skin that no root or leaf could explain, and now, with no mother left to avoid the question for, you are walking toward the answer. You know no one yet. You have done nothing yet. You are only beginning.
